firstSetup.welcomeBody: This Elidune server has not been configured yet. This wizard creates the first administrator account and library details. You will be signed in automatically when done.
firstSetup.libraryHint: Provide at least the library name or the city (required to create the administrator account).
firstSetup.smtpIntro: Optional: SMTP settings for outbound email (reminders, etc.). You can change these later in server settings. Skip if your server does not allow SMTP overrides.

holds.hintBorrowed: This copy is currently on loan. You will be notified when it is available for you.

maintenance.title: Maintenance
maintenance.subtitle: Search index and other administrative tasks
maintenance.meilisearchOn: Meilisearch is configured on this server.
maintenance.meilisearchOff: Meilisearch is not configured on this server.
maintenance.searchIndexingIntro: Catalog search is powered by Meilisearch. The index stores a searchable copy of catalog fields for fast full-text queries.
maintenance.searchIndexingWhen: Run a full reindex after enabling Meilisearch on a new deployment, after bulk data imports (e.g. MARC), migrations, or if search results no longer match the database.
maintenance.searchIndexingAsync: Indexing is asynchronous: this action returns as soon as documents are queued with Meilisearch, not when indexing finishes. Catalogs under about 10,000 items typically complete within a few seconds.
maintenance.z3950Intro: Re-fetches bibliographic metadata by ISBN from a configured Z39.50 server and updates catalog records (relational fields and MARC). Physical items are kept.
maintenance.z3950NoActiveServers: No active Z39.50 server. Configure one under the Z39.50 servers tab.
maintenance.z3950RebuildAllHint: When off: only records with an ISBN and no stored MARC. When on: all active records with an ISBN.
maintenance.actionsGroupIntro: Run actions one by one, or execute the full group in recommended order.

cleanupDanglingBiblioSeries.label: Clean up orphan series links
cleanupDanglingBiblioSeries.description: Removes biblio→series links pointing to non-existent series.

cleanupDanglingBiblioCollections.label: Clean up orphan collection links
cleanupDanglingBiblioCollections.description: Removes biblio→collection links pointing to non-existent collections.

cleanupSeries.label: Clean up series
cleanupSeries.description: Strips quotes around names and deletes series with no bibliographic records.

cleanupCollections.label: Clean up collections
cleanupCollections.description: Strips quotes around names and deletes collections with no bibliographic records.

mergeDuplicateSeries.label: Merge duplicate series
mergeDuplicateSeries.description: Merges duplicate series and moves biblio links.

mergeDuplicateCollections.label: Merge duplicate collections
mergeDuplicateCollections.description: Merges duplicate collections and moves biblio links.

cleanupOrphanAuthors.label: Clean up orphan authors
cleanupOrphanAuthors.description: Deletes authors not referenced by any bibliographic record.

cleanupUsers.label: Clean users
cleanupUsers.description: Removes or updates user accounts according to server maintenance rules.

sources.mergeMinTwo: Select at least 2 sources to merge

sources.mergeHint: Specimens and items from selected sources will be reassigned to the new source. Old sources will be archived.

advanced.title: Saved queries
advanced.subtitle: Design, save, and run flexible statistics (server-driven schema).
advanced.unionWithHelp: Combine this entity with additional root tables that share the same column shape. Joins are still resolved from the selected root entity.
advanced.aggregationsPhysicalOnly: Only physical columns can be aggregated; computed fields are excluded.
advanced.filterGroupsHelp: Combined with the filters above as: (AND of all top filters) AND ((AND of group 1) OR (AND of group 2) OR …). The server enforces limits on group and clause counts.
advanced.emptyOrGroup: No conditions in this group — add one or remove the group.
advanced.timeBucketPhysicalOnly: Time bucketing must use a physical date/timestamp column.
advanced.warnGroupBy: Aggregations are set: add dimensions to GROUP BY if required by the server.

about.title: About Elidune
about.description: Elidune is the library management software developed to meet our specific needs. The server stack includes z3950-rs for the Z39.50 protocol, marc-rs for MARC bibliographic records, and Meilisearch for full-text search.

errors.tooManyRequests: Too many attempts. Please wait before trying again.
errors.network: Network error. Please check your connection.

apiCode.authorization_failed: You do not have permission to perform this action.
apiCode.business_rule_violation: Action refused by a business rule (expired subscription, quota reached, etc.).
apiCode.duplicate_isbn_needs_confirmation: A document with this ISBN already exists.
apiCode.duplicate_barcode_needs_confirmation: A copy with this barcode already exists.
apiCode.database_error: Database error. Please try again later.
apiCode.internal_error: Internal server error. Please try again later.

inventory.scanError: Scan failed (session closed or network error).
inventory.batchError: Import failed (closed session, batch limit, or network).
inventory.scopePlaceInvalid: Invalid place code (non-negative integer or empty).
inventory.sessionHelpBody: Scans belong to this session until it is closed. The report highlights unknown codes, archived copies scanned, duplicates, and gaps in scope.
inventory.sessionHelpOpen: While open, you can keep scanning or importing; the report updates continuously.
inventory.sessionHelpClosed: Session closed: no more scans or imports. Keep the report for follow-up or catalogue fixes.
inventory.scanBarcodeHint: Each read adds a log line; the same barcode may be scanned multiple times (counted as duplicates in the report).
inventory.scanArchived: Known barcode but copy is archived: {{barcode}}.
inventory.batchHint: Paste one barcode per line. The server accepts at most 500 per request; the client splits larger lists automatically.
inventory.reportHint: Totals reflect the session scope: active non-archived items, filtered by place when a place code was set.
inventory.scansTabHintLazy: Chronological order; scroll to the bottom to load more pages.
inventory.missingTabHintLazy: Scroll to load more rows as needed.
inventory.noMissing: No missing copies with the current criteria (or empty scope).
inventory.listHelpBody: A session groups every scan for one stocktake. Scope (all active copies or one place code) is set at creation and cannot be changed later.
inventory.listHelpStep1: Create a session with a clear name; set the place code if you only check one room or store.
inventory.listHelpStep2: While the session is open, scan each barcode or import a list (up to 500 barcodes per server request; the app chunks longer lists).
inventory.listHelpStep3: Review the scan log and the missing list: active in-scope copies that have not yet appeared as a scan in this session.
inventory.listHelpStep4: Close the session to freeze results; the discrepancy report summarises expected counts, unknown codes, and duplicate scans.
inventory.title: Stocktaking
inventory.subtitle: Sessions: scans, discrepancies, and items not yet seen
inventory.createModalHint: Display filter and notes are optional. Only the place code defines the technical scope for counts and lists.
inventory.locationFilterExplain: Informational only; it does not filter items on the server.
inventory.locationFilterHint: e.g. Reading room — free text for staff
inventory.scopePlaceHint: Same numeric `place` as on item records. Leave empty to include all active (non-archived) copies.
inventory.scopePlacePlaceholder: e.g. 3 — leave empty for full scope
inventory.expandUnknownBarcode: No catalogue item matches this barcode.

opac.eventsEmpty: Upcoming library events will appear here.

maintenance.title: Service unavailable
maintenance.body: The application is running, but the database cannot be reached. You cannot use the app right now. Retry after checking PostgreSQL and configuration.

dataController.title: Data Controller
dataController.description: The controller of the personal data collected through this software is:

dataCollected.title: Data Collected
dataCollected.description: In the context of using the Elidune library management software, the following personal data is collected:
dataCollected.items: Last name, first name, username, email address, account type (member, librarian, administrator), loan and return history, system activity (audit log).

purpose.title: Purposes of Processing
purpose.description: Data is processed for the following purposes:
purpose.items: User account management and authentication, loan and return tracking, library administration, anonymised usage statistics, sending reminders for overdue loans.

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis
legalBasis.description: Processing is based on the performance of a task in the public interest or in the exercise of official authority (Article 6.1.e GDPR), as well as on the contract between the user and the library (Article 6.1.b).

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.description: Data is retained for the duration of the library membership and deleted within a reasonable period after account closure, unless a longer retention period is legally required.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.description: In accordance with the GDPR, you have the following rights:
rights.items: Right of access, right to rectification, right to erasure (right to be forgotten), right to restriction of processing, right to object, right to data portability.

contact.title: Exercising Your Rights
contact.description: To exercise your rights or for any question regarding the protection of your personal data, contact the data controller:

security.title: Security
security.description: Data is stored locally on the library's server. Appropriate technical measures are implemented to ensure the confidentiality and integrity of data (username/password authentication, password encryption, audit log).

noThirdParty.title: No Third-Party Transfer
noThirdParty.description: Personal data is not transmitted to third parties, nor transferred outside the library's internal network.
